breaking_news.title: Fortinet FortiClient EMS Critical SQL Injection Flaw Actively Exploited
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers are actively exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient Endpoint Management Server. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-21643, allows unauthenticated threat actors to execute arbitrary code on unpatched systems via crafted HTTP requests.

This incident affects over 900,000 customers relying on Fortinet for cybersecurity management, posing a severe risk to enterprise endpoint security. The rapid exploitation highlights a concerning trend of repeated vulnerabilities within the vendor's ecosystem, necessitating immediate patching.

breaking_news.title: Citrix NetScaler Memory Overread Vulnerability Under Active Exploitation
breaking_news.summary:
Hackers are exploiting a critical memory overread vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway appliances to obtain sensitive data. Tracked as CVE-2026-3055, the flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to leak application memory and potentially gain administrative session IDs.

Organizations using Citrix NetScaler for secure remote access are at immediate risk of data leakage and unauthorized access. The active exploitation in the wild underscores the urgency for administrators to apply security updates to prevent widespread compromise.

breaking_news.title: F5 BIG-IP APM DoS Flaw Reclassified as Critical RCE and Exploited
breaking_news.summary:
F5 has reclassified a BIG-IP APM denial-of-service vulnerability as a critical remote code execution flaw following evidence of active exploitation. Attackers are now deploying webshells on unpatched devices using the vulnerability tracked as CVE-2025-53521.

This escalation impacts a vast number of enterprises relying on F5 infrastructure for application delivery and security. CISA has added the flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, signaling a critical need for immediate remediation across the sector.

breaking_news.title: 375TB of Lockheed Martin Data Listed on Dark Web by APT Iran
breaking_news.summary:
A dark web market is listing 375TB of alleged Lockheed Martin data for $600 million, claiming the leak was provided by a group calling itself APT Iran. The massive dataset reportedly contains sensitive defense contractor information and intellectual property.

If verified, this represents one of the largest data exfiltrations targeting a major defense contractor in recent history. The incident highlights the persistent threat of state-sponsored actors targeting critical defense infrastructure for espionage and leverage.

breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Hackers Breach FBI Director Kash Patel's Email
breaking_news.summary:
Iran-linked hackers from the Handala group successfully breached the personal email account of FBI Director Kash Patel. The group leaked photos and documents to the internet, prompting the FBI to confirm the incident and offer a $10 million reward for information.

While the FBI states no government data was exposed, the targeting of a high-profile law enforcement official signals an escalation in state-sponsored harassment campaigns. This event underscores the personal security risks faced by government leaders in the current geopolitical climate.

breaking_news.title: European Commission Confirms 350GB Data Breach by ShinyHunters
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its Europa.eu web platform, with the ShinyHunters extortion group claiming to have stolen over 350GB of data. The breach involved cloud systems hosting websites and services for EU institutions.

This incident compromises sensitive internal communications and potentially confidential documents within the European Union's central online infrastructure. The attack demonstrates the vulnerability of government cloud environments to organized cybercrime groups seeking ransom or data exposure.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Targets Telnyx SDK in PyPI Supply Chain Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The cybercrime crew TeamPCP has uploaded malicious versions of the Telnyx Python SDK to the PyPI registry to steal cloud and crypto credentials. This supply chain attack follows similar compromises of LiteLLM and Trivy packages, targeting developers across multiple operating systems.

Developers integrating the Telnyx SDK into their applications are at risk of credential theft and unauthorized access to their cloud infrastructure. The attack illustrates the growing sophistication of supply chain compromises targeting popular development tools and libraries.

breaking_news.title: Russian APT TA446 Deploys DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit in Phishing Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Russian state-sponsored group TA446 is leveraging the DarkSword iOS exploit kit in targeted spear-phishing campaigns against iPhone users. The campaign targets government, higher education, and financial entities, utilizing leaked exploit tools to compromise mobile devices.

This activity marks a significant shift in mobile threat landscapes as APT groups adopt advanced exploit kits for iOS targeting. Organizations with mobile workforces must enhance their email security and device management policies to mitigate these sophisticated intrusion attempts.
